hints.Deus: 'Deus' is a noun meaning 'God' — it is the subject
hints.mundum: 'mundum' is a noun in the accusative meaning 'the world'

hints.Present: Present of 'diligere' 3rd person would be 'diligit' — 'dilexit' has the perfect -x- stem
hints.Imperfect: Imperfect of 'diligere' would be 'diligebat' — 'dilexit' has the perfect -xit ending
hints.Pluperfect: Pluperfect of 'diligere' would be 'dilexerat' — 'dilexit' lacks the -era- marker

hints.lego, legere, legi, lectum: These are the principal parts of 'legere' (to read/choose)
hints.eligo, eligere, elegi, electum: These are the principal parts of 'eligere' (to choose/select)
hints.amo, amare, amavi, amatum: These are the principal parts of 'amare' (to love) — a different verb for love

hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'Deus' would be 'Dei' — 'Deus' is the nominative form
hints.Accusative: Accusative of 'Deus' would be 'Deum' — 'Deus' is the nominative form
hints.Vocative: Vocative of 'Deus' would be 'Deus' (irregular) — but here it is the subject, not an address

hints.Nominative: Nominative of 'mundus' is 'mundus' — 'mundum' has the -um accusative ending
hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'mundus' would be 'mundi' — 'mundum' ends in -um
hints.Ablative: Ablative of 'mundus' would be 'mundo' — 'mundum' has the -um accusative ending

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'mundi' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'mundi' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -ūs — 'mundi' ends in -i

hints.Conjunction: Conjunctions join clauses (like 'et', 'sed') — 'Sic' modifies the verb 'dilexit'
hints.Preposition: Prepositions govern nouns (like 'in', 'ad') — 'Sic' modifies the verb
hints.Pronoun: Pronouns replace nouns — 'Sic' is an adverb meaning 'so/thus'
